message: Last week my kids asked why we were putting batteries in the basement 'like we're doomsday preppers.' I laughed and said, 'Nope—just being ready so we can actually *enjoy* movie night if the power goes out instead of panicking.' Their faces lit up. Suddenly organizing our storm kit became less 'chores Mom makes us do' and more 'survival adventure prep.' Now my 9-year-old treats our weather check-ins like a game. Funny how reframing 'boring emergency planning' as 'let's be the family that handles storms like pros' makes everyone way more into it. Have you found a way to get your household actually *excited* about being prepared, instead of treating it like a drag?
